Daniel L. Balsam, Esq. and Jacob Harker, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Erik Hellman, who was placed under oath and testified.

No appearance by defendants Real Bright Media, Inc., a California corporation, or Joshua Bass, an individual.

Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint for Violations of California Restrictions of Unsolicited Commerical Emails {California Bus. & Prof. Code Section 17529.5} was filed on September 24, 2015.
Default as to defendant Joshua Bass, an individual, was entered on June 29, 2016.
Default as to defendant Real Bright Media, Inc., a California corporation, was entered on March 23, 2021

Damages/Principal - $388,000.00.
Costs - $4,558.63.

Total - $392,558.63.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS MARY O'SHEA, MARGIE BARR AND DANIEL BARRETT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To 75 Emails And Real Bright Medias Defenses is Off calendar. Plaintiff filed the instant motion in July 2015. Plaintiff filed its First Amended Complaint on September 24, 2015. " ' "[A]n amendatory pleading supersedes the original one, which ceases to perform any function as a pleading." ' " (Foreman & Clark Corp. v. Fallon (1971) 3 Cal.3d 875, 884, 92 Cal.Rptr. 162, 479 P.2d 362.) "The amended complaint furnishes the sole basis for the cause of action, and the original complaint ceases to have any effect either as a pleading or as a basis for judgment. [Citation.] [] Because there is but one complaint in a civil action [citation], the filing of an amended complaint moots a motion directed to a prior complaint. [Citation.]" (State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131, 109 Cal.Rptr.3d 88.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

RULING - NOTICE WAS GIVEN TO THE OPPOSING PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS MARGIE BARR, DANIEL BARRETT and JASON BISHOP'S Motion To Quash Defendants Subpoena To Yahoo And AT&T Internet Services, And Request For Monetary Sanctions - The motion to quash is GRANTED in its entirety. Defendant argues it needs the information of six former plaintiffs and one who was never a party to help support its defense that the Plaintiffs did not actually click on the alleged spam links. The Court finds there is no rational reason to obtain this information in regard to people who are not actually parties to this litigation. (See CCP 2017.010.) As to the subpoenas related to current Plaintiffs, the court finds they are overbroad. Plaintifs request for damages for violation of the Stored Communications Act is DENIED. There is no cause of action in this case for violation under the SCA. Attorneys' Fee in the amount of $8,400 and Cost in the amount of $594.00 are granted. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
